sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation


The current workspaceFolder in dev container is wrong. We should just remove it and use the default setting.

Reason: The local workspace is mounted to /workspace/sglang but vscode will open /sgl-workspace/sglang/, which is actually the pre-installed sglang from the base image instead o

sglang.title: fix missing revision arg when loading tokenizer
sglang.author: giorgiopiatti
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Currently the revision arg is only used when loading a model and not the tokenizer, so if a HF repo does not have a tokenizer in revision=main the script crashes.
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model  ${MODEL_REPO} --revision ${MODEL_REVISION}
Modifications
Changed the loading of token

sglang.title: [#2812] Make the decode status dict capcity adjustable by a CLI param
sglang.author: seungduk-yanolja
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Running SGLang with a high-end GPU and a small size model crashes sometimes and it turned out to be a problem of the detokenizer's state dictionary size.

Modifications

Doubled the dict size.
Made the dict size to be configurable using an env var (DETOKENIZER_MAX_STATES).


Checklist

 F

sglang.author: hongpeng-guo

This PR tries to support custom logits processor registered by users, so users can easily implement their custom sampling methods without the need to change the sglang code.
Related Issue
#2291

Modifications

Introduce the common abstract interface CustomLogitProcessor as in sglang/srt/s

sglang.title: Move sgl.Runtime under sglang/lang
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
sgl.Runtime is mostly used by the language frontend. For other use cases, we should go with sgl.Engine.
This is a sub pr of #2959

Previously we iteratively used outlines and X-Grammar in structured-output docs. Now we only use xgrammar since xgrammar supports regex now.

sglang.title: Separate two entry points: Engine and HTTP server
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
We want to separate the Engine and HTTP server, so we can use the two entrypoints flexibly without depending on each other.
This PR splits the file python/sglang/srt/server.py into the following two files:

Engine: python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/engine.py

Engine class, _launch_subprocesses


HTTP se

vllm.title: [torch.compile] store inductor compiled Python file
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#11108 enabled the compilation cache, but it only stores a hash string, which is not human-readable.
this PR further stores the path of the python file compiled by inductor, so that we can manually check the result.
As a result, I can confirm that torch.compile does not do anything w.r.t. kv cache,

vllm.title: [Model] Support for fairseq2 Llama
vllm.author: MartinGleize
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support for all Llama models produced with fairseq2. Its checkpoint format is mostly the same as the original Llama checkpoint format, up to layer renaming.
The PR provides a model with custom weight loading, and adds Fairseq2LlamaForCausalLM to the model registry.
Loading of sharded we

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix num_heads value for simple connector when tp enabled
vllm.author: ShangmingCai
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a bug fix for #11058. The former fix did not handle the head_size properly when tp is enabled.
Let say, if -tp 2 is enabled, we will send the key/value cache with its shape equal to "[num_layer, prompt_token_length, num_key_value_heads, head_size]" in the implementation of #11058. And the "[

vllm.title: [torch.compile] fix sym_tensor_indices
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Previously, this variable is calculated incorrectly. It contains all tensors (including weights).

vllm.title: Move linting to `pre-commit`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Using pre-commit for linting, formatting and type checking:

Significantly reduces the amount of code used for formatting, making it more maintainable
Leverages the rich pre-commit ecosystem
Performs checks at commit time, reducing the chance of check failing code being pushed and wasting CI time

vllm.title: [Core] Interface for accessing model from `VllmRunner`
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds a new method LLM.apply_model to apply a function to the model in each worker without having to access  llm.llm_engine.model_executor.driver_worker.model_runner.model.
FIX #4084
